title: Verb Conjugation and Stem Changes
description: Learn common Spanish verbs with stem changes and their conjugations.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Comenzar means 'to commence' or 'to start'. Another common verb for 'to start' is empezar. Empezar has a stem change in Spain, pronounced empe/th/ar.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How do you say 'I start' using empezar?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Empiezo. Good. Remember to go back to the 'to' form, empezar, to avoid guesswork. How do you say 'he starts'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Empiezas. Good. How do you say 'when are you starting?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Cuándo empiezas? Very good. In the present tense, the e can split to ie, and the o can split to ue. This happens because of the stress on the penultimate syllable. For example, poder becomes puedo. How do you say 'I sleep'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Duermo. Good. To say 'I fall asleep', you say 'me duermo'. How do you say 'he sleeps'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Duermen. Good. Encontrar means 'to encounter' or 'to find'. How do you say 'I find it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Lo encuentro. Good. Mostrar means 'to show'. How do you say 'I show'?

outro.text: You've learned about stem-changing verbs in Spanish, like empezar and dormir. These changes occur in the present tense.
Try conjugating other stem-changing verbs like poder or volver. Remember the vowel changes in the stem.
Understanding these patterns helps you predict and use many Spanish verbs correctly.
